root_causes.title: Croner Snooze and Schedule UI Gaps
root_causes.description: Backend scheduler supports snooze and weekly/monthly options, but UI lacks buttons and form inputs to use them.
root_causes.solution_summary: Add an onSnooze action to ReminderRow and implement toggle snooze method. Expand ScheduleForm with weekly and monthly options.

root_causes.title: Missing viewport ref for Auto-scroll
root_causes.description: ActivityFeed does not auto-scroll on new messages because the ScrollArea lacks a ref that can be manipulated.
root_causes.solution_summary: Expose a ref in ScrollArea component and use it in a useEffect within ActivityFeed to scrollTo bottom when activities update.

root_causes.title: Robust MIME-type handling in MediaArtifact
root_causes.description: MediaItemDownloader renders all non-video/audio formats as tags.
root_causes.solution_summary: Explicitly check `format.startsWith('image/')` for images and provide a fallback UI rendering for unsupported formats.

root_causes.title: Hardcoded Windows file paths for Snippets
root_causes.description: Snippets feature breaks due to hardcoded 'D:/fuse' paths.
root_causes.solution_summary: Update MANIFEST_PATH and FUSE_ROOT defaults to use the app's userData directory.

root_causes.title: Missing SDK IPC Exposure
root_causes.description: Cache inspection functions defined in Jules SDK are missing from the IPC bridge.
root_causes.solution_summary: Expose getCacheInfo, getSessionCacheInfo, getActivityCount, getLatestActivities, and getSessionCount.

tasks.title: Add missing SDK cache methods to IPC
tasks.prompt:
The `jules-sdk` has several cache-related functions that are not currently exposed through the IPC bridge, making them inaccessible to the frontend renderer. 

Please expose the following SDK functions in the IPC bridge:
- `jules.getCacheInfo(rootDirOverride?)`
- `jules.getSessionCacheInfo(sessionId, rootDirOverride?)`
- `jules.getActivityCount(sessionId, rootDirOverride?)`
- `jules.getLatestActivities(sessionId, n, rootDirOverride?)`
- `jules.getSessionCount(rootDirOverride?)`

1. Modify `electron/ipc/handlers.ts` to add `ipcMain.handle` endpoints (e.g. `sdk:cache.getCacheInfo`, `sdk:cache.getSessionCacheInfo`, etc.) that call the underlying `jules` instance functions. You may need to import the functions from `@google/jules-sdk` if they are standalone rather than methods on the client, or invoke them from `jules.xxx` if they exist there.
2. Update `src/jules/sdk-ipc.ts` to define the `cache` object under `SdkIpc` with the appropriate TypeScript types for these 5 functions.
3. Update `electron/ipc/bridge.ts` to implement the `sdk.cache` property mapping the functions to their respective `invoke('sdk:cache.xxx')` calls.

Acceptance criteria:
- The 5 cache functions are accurately typed and exposed in the renderer via `sdkIpc.cache`.
- You may ONLY modify the files listed above. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

tasks.title: Robust MIME-type handling in MediaArtifact
tasks.prompt: Currently in `src/renderer/components/workspace/activity/activity-artifacts.tsx`, the `MediaItemDownloader` explicitly checks if `media.format.startsWith('video/')` or `audio/`. If it doesn't match either, it blindly assumes it is an image and renders an `` tag. ```tsx // Current behavior: {media.format.startsWith('video/') ? (

tasks.title: Auto-scroll Activity Feed
tasks.prompt:
In `src/renderer/components/workspace/activity/activity-feed.tsx`, the `ScrollArea` does not automatically scroll to the bottom when new activities stream in, unlike `ActivityPage.tsx` which manually controls `el.scrollTo`.

1. Modify `src/renderer/ui/scroll-area.tsx` to forward a `viewportRef` to the `ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport` component so the parent can access the raw DOM element.
   ```tsx
   export const ScrollArea = React.forwardRef<HTMLDivElement, ...>(({ className, children, viewportRef, ...props }, ref) => {
       return <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Root ref={ref}>
           <ScrollAreaPrimitive.Viewport ref={viewportRef}>
           ...
   ```
2. Update `ActivityFeed.tsx` to pass a `viewportRef` to the `ScrollArea` component.
3. Add a `useEffect` inside `ActivityFeed.tsx` that triggers auto-scrolling to the bottom (`el.scrollTo({top: el.scrollHeight, behavior: 'smooth'})`) whenever the `activities` array changes.

Acceptance criteria:
- The `ScrollArea` viewport can be referenced from `ActivityFeed`.
- `ActivityFeed` smooth scrolls to the bottom on new messages.
- You may ONLY modify the files listed above. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

tasks.title: Add Snooze to Reminders UI
tasks.prompt:
The backend `scheduler` in Electron supports `snooze(id, minutes)` and robust scheduling kinds like `weekly` and `monthly`, but the frontend UI does not expose these features.

1. In `src/renderer/pages/electron/RemindersPage.tsx`:
   - In `ReminderRow`, add a snooze button (e.g. an alarm clock icon or Zzz) next to the toggle/remove buttons, triggered by an `onSnooze` property.
   - In `RemindersPage`, implement a `snooze(id: string)` method that calls `scheduler.snooze(id, 15)` and adds the returned item to the `items` state, then pass this into `onSnooze`.
2. In `src/renderer/components/shared/ScheduleForm.tsx`:
   - Expand the `Kind` type to include `weekly` and `monthly`.
   - Add UI inputs for `weekly` (e.g., a select for Day of Week `0-6`) and `monthly` (e.g., an input for Day of Month `1-31`).
   - Update the `buildSchedule` logic to map these to the `ScheduleInput` type expected by the bridge.

Acceptance criteria:
- Reminders can be snoozed from the UI.
- Users can create weekly and monthly schedules from the form.
- You may ONLY modify the files listed above. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.

tasks.title: Fix hardcoded snippet paths
tasks.prompt:
Snippets fail to save properly because the paths are hardcoded to `D:/fuse`, which breaks on most environments.

1. In `src/shared/fuse.ts`, modify `FUSE_ROOT`. Since this is shared between Node and Browser, you could change it to an empty string `''` or a generic string, but the primary fix is in the backend.
2. In `electron/snippets.ts`, replace the hardcoded `const MANIFEST_PATH = 'D:/fuse/snippets.json'` and the default fallback of `getFuseRoot()` to dynamically resolve inside the Electron user data directory (`app.getPath('userData')`). 
   - For example: `const getFuseRoot = () => store.get('fuse.root') || path.join(app.getPath('userData'), 'fuse')`.
   - Ensure `MANIFEST_PATH` dynamically uses `path.join(getFuseRoot(), 'snippets.json')` so that files are correctly saved to the user's OS-specific app data folder instead of the hardcoded D drive.

Acceptance criteria:
- Snippets storage path dynamically uses the user data directory instead of a hardcoded D drive path.
- You may ONLY modify the files listed above. Do NOT rename, move, or delete any files outside your boundary.
